_instructions: Fill this file with 15-30 question/answer pairs from YOUR corpus.
For each question, mark which chunk(s) are the GOLD (truly relevant) chunks.
You can reference gold chunks in EITHER of two ways (mix freely):
2. gold_chunk_ids: the raw sha256 chunk ids.
To discover the source/chunk_index/id of every stored chunk, run:
gold_answer is OPTIONAL: provide it to enable Exact-Match / F1 scoring.
The '_instructions' and '_note' keys are ignored by the eval harness.
The examples below are wired to the bundled sample_corpus so `run_eval` works out of the box.
_note: Delete the examples and add your own once you ingest your corpus. Keep the same shape.

questions.question: What does a cosine similarity of 1.0 between two vectors mean?
questions.gold_answer: The two vectors point in the same direction.

questions.question: Why is ChromaDB a low-cost vector store compared to a fully managed one?
questions.gold_answer: It runs embedded on the local filesystem with no always-on pods, so cost is essentially the disk and VM you already run.

questions.question: How does deriving a chunk id from its content make re-ingestion idempotent?
questions.gold_answer: Because identical content always hashes to the same id, so inserting it again is detected and skipped, creating no duplicate vectors.

questions.question: What should a RAG system do when no retrieved chunk is relevant to the question?
questions.gold_answer: It should say it has no relevant context rather than fabricate an answer.

questions.question: Which metrics are used to measure retrieval quality in RAG?
questions.gold_answer: Recall@k, Mean Reciprocal Rank (MRR), and normalized Discounted Cumulative Gain (nDCG).
